subject: 🏃 Wednesday — Mobility Deep-Dive + Upper Pull
html:
Today: Mobility Deep-Dive (~35 min) + Upper Pull (~20 min). Day 3 of Week 1 in Chassis Build — 3.5 weeks to your May 15 PT follow-up. This is the biggest mobility session of the week, targeting the bilateral hip IR limitation the PT flagged as the primary driver of your knee issues. Dead bug with banded pulldown and chin tuck + lifts are built into the protocol — that's your third PT-prescribed core session this week.

Mobility Deep-Dive Protocol:

  1. Foam roll full lower body — 5 min
  2. Foam roller T-spine extensions (PT) — 10 reps
  3. Quadruped banded hip IR / lateral band pull (PT) — 8 reps each side
  4. Standing lunges with hip IR + extension (PT) — 10 reps each side
  5. Seated 90/90 flow — 5 min (switch sides every 30 sec, add forward fold over front knee)
  6. Couch stretch — 90 sec each side
  7. Pigeon pose — 90 sec each side
  8. Banded hip distraction — 2 min each side
  9. Standing hip CARs — 5 each direction, each leg
  10. Quadruped thread-the-needle — 10 each side
  11. Dead bug with banded pulldown (PT) — 2 sets × 10 marches
  12. Chin tuck + lifts (PT) — 2 reps each direction (front, diag L, diag R), 3-sec hold

Upper Pull — warm up first: arm circles (10 each direction), shoulder rolls (10 forward/back), scapular retractions (10), cat-cow (10), band pull-aparts (10). Then 3×10–12 on each:

ExerciseWeight
Pull-upsBodyweight (lat pulldown 85 lbs if <8 reps)
Dumbbell row30 lbs/hand
Face pullsLight band / 20 lbs cable
Bicep curls15 lbs/hand
Band pull-apartsLight band

Focus cue: During the 90/90 flow, pay attention to which side has less internal rotation range. Note any left-vs-right asymmetry — that's the primary progress signal the PT will want at your follow-up.

Adaptation notes: I don't have logs from Monday (Hip & Core A) or Tuesday (3-mi run) yet. Your last reported knee status was Yellow on April 16, six days ago. Let me know how the first two days went and where the knee is — if it's been green, the Chassis Build is on track.
